sourceMovie.title: Double Jeopardy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Double Jeopardy for the same reason it broke through in 1999: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Bruce Beresford keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Woman in Cabin 10
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Double Jeopardy clicked for you, The Woman in Cabin 10 (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Double Jeopardy?
faq.answer: Try The Woman in Cabin 10, High Crimes, The Limey—each shares DNA with Double Jeopardy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Double Jeopardy?
faq.answer: Double Jeopardy (1999) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Double Jeopardy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
